ending: Jin and Meiling complete their marriage at Fa Ram and begin spring together. Meiling awakens usable chi and discovers that the living land responds to her as it does to Jin. Bi De, Chun Ke, Pi Pa, Ri Zu, Tigu, and Wa Shi remain by choice as the household's animal students and defenders. Yun Ren and Gou Ren have also awakened chi without abandoning family or work, and Gou Ren plans to spend the summer at the farm. Cai Xiulan preserves Fa Ram's secrecy, still owes Jin and Meiling a life debt, and receives permission to stay temporarily as a learner. Chow Ji and Sun Ken are dead, with Cai Xiulan publicly credited for Sun Ken's defeat. Luban and a confined Shrouded Mountain disciple remain unresolved dangers, while the vast solstice fire formation remains unexplained. Beneath these human concerns, Fa Ram's long-injured land spirit has recovered enough to become self-aware. It trusts Jin and Meiling and begins exploring its domain in a joyful but incomplete new form.
in_short: An outsider awakens in the dying body of Jin Jue and rejects sect life, settling as Jin on a farm he names Fa Ram. His practical use of chi makes the land flourish and awakens several animals, led by the rooster Bi De. Jin becomes part of Hong Yaowu and falls in love with its healer, Meiling. The household survives Chow Ji's attempt to corrupt Bi De and Sun Ken's raid, while Jin accepts the animals as free students and protectors. Meiling and Jin save Cai Xiulan, who conceals Fa Ram's power and accepts public credit for Sun Ken's death. The Song brothers awaken chi but retain their ordinary commitments. Jin and Meiling marry at Fa Ram, where Meiling gains chi and a bond with the land. The closing revelation is that Jin's care has healed the farm's ancient wounded spirit enough for it to awaken as a weak, benevolent consciousness linked to the couple.

recaps.text:
An outsider consciousness awakens in the mortally injured body of Jin Jue, a low-ranking Cloudy Sword Sect disciple. Rejecting the dead man's desire for revenge, he leaves Crimson Crucible City as Jin and buys land in the quiet Azure Hills. He establishes Fa Ram near Verdant Hill, using chi for construction, crops, and spirit herbs rather than advancement through combat. His rooster Big D awakens as the cultivating Bi De and becomes the farm's vigilant defender, killing the fox Basi Bu Shi during Jin's absence.

In Hong Yaowu, Jin meets the healer Meiling, her friend Meihua, and their families. Meiling senses immense but life-filled chi in him. Jin kills the feared Reaper Wolf and later protects Meihua from a man claiming to be Zhang Li of the Shrouded Mountain Sect. Jin defeats the claimant and his companions with ease, exposing more strength than he knew he possessed. His friendship with Meiling becomes a courtship, and he plans a larger home with marriage in mind. Ting Feng gives him the pigs Chun Ke and Pi Pa in gratitude. A sect elder later reveals that the supposed Zhang Li is actually Luban, an infiltrator who murdered the real young master and stole his identity. Luban retains his disguise and carries a grievance against Jin.

Fa Ram produces an extraordinary rice harvest, and Meiling, Yun Ren, and Gou Ren help Jin bring it in. Meiling recognizes Bi De as a spirit beast, while Hong Xian accepts Jin as a trusted friend of Hong Yaowu. At the Mid-Autumn Festival, Jin asks to marry Meiling once he can provide for her. Hong Xian approves, and their spring betrothal becomes public. Jin also confronts the troubling scale of his own power and tells Meiling about leaving the Cloudy Sword Sect after abuse and near death. With her support, he renews his commitment to protect a home rather than seek fights. He acquires an orange kitten and names her Tigu.

During Jin's absences, Chow Ji leads a starving Blackfur group onto the farm. Bi De admits them as guests and accepts pills refined by Chow Ji, believing the increased strength will help him defend Fa Ram. Chow Ji instead exploits his ambition, consumes the farm's resources, and places a corrupting control technique inside him. A small female Blackfur recognizes the danger. When Bi De finally sees the damage to the farm, he resists, but Chow Ji incapacitates him and injures Chun Ke and Pi Pa. The small Blackfur shields Bi De from the internal attack, allowing him to rise and kill Chow Ji. The leader's final curse leaves surviving Blackfurs unstable, the animals badly hurt, and Fa Ram damaged.

Jin returns to find Fa Ram damaged and its defenders wounded. He destroys Chow Ji's remaining pills and furnace, accepts the helpful female Blackfur as a resident, and realizes that his animals may be people rather than livestock. The rat Ri Zu helps Bi De purge the lingering corruption, while Meiling's medicine aids Chun Ke's slow recovery. Jin builds a large river-integrated house for the coming winter. The spirit within Fa Ram accepts chi from Bi De before becoming dormant under the snow.

Tigu joins the household's defense and, after challenging Bi De, begins accepting his instruction. Ri Zu asks Meiling to teach her medicine, and Gou Ren asks to learn farming from Jin. Meanwhile, the Whirling Demon Sword gang discovers the farm. When a scout attacks Bi De, the rooster leads Tigu, Chun Ke, Pi Pa, and Ri Zu against roughly thirty raiders while Jin and his human guests remain unaware. The animals rout the bandits, and Bi De kills their leader Sun Ken after a difficult duel. On their return, Ri Zu explains that the gang intended to harm Jin, Meiling, the Song brothers, and Fa Ram. Jin is disturbed by the deaths but does not reject his defenders. He accepts responsibility for guiding them and resolves to teach them how to use their growing power in service of a peaceful life.

Cai Xiulan of the Verdant Blade Sect follows Sun Ken's trail while suffering from grave wounds and demonic contamination. She reaches Fa Ram and collapses. Meiling directs an extraction formation, Ri Zu assists, and Jin supplies the stable chi needed to remove the corruption. As Cai Xiulan recovers, Jin gathers the animal defenders and formally recognizes their agency. He offers them the freedom to leave or remain and explains why he values a good, compassionate life above power or immortality. They choose to stay. Bi De accepts this path and undergoes a cultivation breakthrough.

Cai Xiulan wakes fully healed and swears a life debt to Jin and Meiling. She learns that Sun Ken is dead and interprets Fa Ram as the domain of a concealed master. Jin gives her Sun Ken's sword as proof and allows her to receive credit, protecting the animals from outside attention. Meiling teaches her the healing formation, and Cai Xiulan learns to regard the farm's spirit beasts as allies. She assesses Bi De as having reached the Profound Realm. During an ice-fishing outing, a food-seeking sentient carp named Wa Shi reveals himself. Jin's lessons in balance, perception, and connection help Cai Xiulan advance to the fourth stage of the Initiate Realm. Before leaving, she promises to preserve Fa Ram's privacy and accepts an invitation to the spring wedding.

Meiling returns to Hong Yaowu with several of Fa Ram's animals, and Hong Xian receives them as guests. Cai Xiulan reaches the Verdant Blade Sect with Sun Ken's sword and is publicly credited with killing him. In private, she tells her father that a hidden master saved her and was responsible for Sun Ken's defeat. Her father agrees to protect Jin's location and to have the captured weapon remade into the plow Jin had mentioned. Verdant Hill's officials also learn that Sun Ken is already dead, relieving them of the immediate threat, while continuing to treat Jin with careful respect.

Jin obtains a recording crystal and prepares a solstice celebration for Hong Yaowu. At Fa Ram, he teaches his animal disciples about the longest night, makes decorated food with them, and records their gathering. A confined Shrouded Mountain disciple remains an unresolved danger, advancing despite punishment and intending to kill Jin when able. Jin then brings gifts to Hong Yaowu's children and personal presents for Meiling and his household. During the village's all-night observance, Hong Xian dances within a ring of fires while the community tends the ritual. Bi De perceives that the fires connect with distant points in a vast formation spanning the landscape, interrupted by unexplained dark gaps. Its purpose remains unknown when the rite ends at sunrise.

Winter continues with household work and preparation for the spring wedding. Jin begins structured lessons for Bi De, Chun Ke, Pi Pa, Tigu, and Wa Shi, teaching literacy, arithmetic, and practical inquiry while adapting to their different needs. He also confronts the moral difference between ordinary farm animals and the awakened beings he treats as people. Tigu remains ambitious but chooses to stay because she sees Fa Ram's protection as her duty. Jin builds a substantial ice store after experiments with chi fail to preserve ice effectively.

Hong Xian, Yun Ren, Gou Ren, and a blacksmith relative help Jin finish the new house. Jin accepts their assistance and strengthens his place within Meiling's family. During the work, the Song brothers repeatedly break tools and show abnormal endurance. Jin identifies uncontrolled chi as the cause and begins teaching them control. He explains opened meridians and the dantian, then turns maple tapping and syrup production into sustained practical training. After nine days, Yun Ren and Gou Ren consciously awaken their chi. Jin asks what they intend to do with cultivation. Both choose to keep their family and work commitments instead of pursuing sect status or military careers. Bi De accepts them as junior disciples and helps them sense the waking land and the reciprocal bond between people and the place they tend.

Yun Ren and Gou Ren return home as newly awakened chi-users, and their parents accept that they will retain their ordinary plans. Gou Ren intends to work at Fa Ram during the summer. Wedding preparations gather pace. Yun Ren learns to operate Jin's recording crystal, while Cai Xiulan evades watchers from her sect and arrives with a cart of gifts. Bi De ceremonially welcomes the procession, and Verdant Hill's lord magistrate oversees the exchange between Jin and Hong Xian.

Jin and Meiling marry at Fa Ram. Their celebration includes gifts, music, recorded images, and a public acknowledgment of the household they have built. When their chi mingles, the land responds to Meiling. She wakes with usable chi and a reciprocal affinity with Fa Ram. Cai Xiulan asks to remain temporarily and learn from the couple, and the household begins spring with Jin, Meiling, their animal students, Gou Ren, and Cai Xiulan together.

The final perspective reveals that Fa Ram contains an ancient, badly wounded consciousness. Jin's steady care has healed its fracture without exploiting it, and the energies it received over the seasons have helped it recover. During winter sleep, Jin's strengthening bond accidentally releases a stored tainted fragment, allowing a small self-aware portion of the land spirit to reform. Still weak and incomplete, it recognizes Jin and Meiling as trusted connections and begins exploring its domain. Chow Ji and Sun Ken are dead, but Luban and a hostile confined disciple remain future threats.
